# UC-17.1.69 · TEAP (Tunnel EAP) Authentication Mode Tracking and Inner-Method Failure Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*TEAP authenticates both the device and the user at the same time — like requiring both a building badge and an ID. We watch how often this works versus fails to make sure the strong authentication is rolling out smoothly.*

---

## Description

Tracks the share of TEAP authentications versus other EAP methods, decomposes TEAP into its inner methods (EAP-TLS, MS-CHAPv2), and detects high failure rates per inner method or per chaining result (user-only vs. machine-only vs. both).

## Value

TEAP enables true user+machine attestation for Zero Trust. Tracking adoption shows progress; tracking failure rates flags integration issues (machine cert problems, user identity store failures, switch-side TEAP support gaps).

## Implementation

Schedule every 1h. TEAP requires Windows 11 22H2+ or Cisco Secure Client native, plus switch IOS-XE 17.x+ supplicant and ISE 3.0+ server.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Disable user cert on TEAP supplicant. Inner-method failures spike. UC-17.1.69 fires.

### Negative scenario

Working TEAP. Low failure rate; chaining result mostly 'user-and-machine'.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" EapAuthentication="EAP-TEAP"
| stats count, count(eval(MessageCode=="5400")) as failures by EapInnerMethod, EapChainingResult
| where failures > 50
```

## Visualization

(1) TEAP adoption percentage trend. (2) Inner-method failure breakdown. (3) Chaining-result distribution (user-and-machine vs. user-only vs. machine-only).

## Known False Positives

- Initial TEAP rollout — high failures during supplicant config drift.
- Machine cert renewal cycles cause temporary failures.

## References

- [RFC 7170 — TEAP](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7170)
- [Cisco ISE TEAP Configuration](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
